task_completion.description: L-shaped base profile is sketched with correct dimensions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 2D L-shaped sketch in the SolveSpace canvas. Visible dimensions should include 60 (horizontal length), 80 (vertical length), and 10 (thickness of the legs).

task_completion.description: L-shaped base is extruded symmetrically to 40mm
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 2D sketch turned into a 3D L-bracket. The property browser should indicate an extrusion with a total width of 40mm (or a symmetric extrusion of 20mm on each side).

task_completion.description: Triangular stiffening rib is sketched with correct dimensions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a triangular sketch on the inner corner of the L-bracket. Visible dimensions or constraints should indicate the vertices are at (10,10), (45,10), and (10,60), meaning the triangle legs are 35mm and 50mm long.

task_completion.description: Triangular rib is extruded to 6mm thickness and centered
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 3D triangular rib connecting the two legs of the bracket. The property browser should show an extrusion thickness of 6mm (or 3mm symmetric) and it should appear visually centered on the 40mm wide bracket.

Look at the Property Browser window (usually top left). The active groups should display 'ok' (e.g., 'Group 2 - ok'), indicating zero degrees of freedom. Sketch lines should appear in the fully constrained color (typically green in SolveSpace).

task_completion.description: The model is saved to the correct file path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the SolveSpace window title bar or a 'Save As' dialog box. It should display 'ribbed_bracket.slvs' or the full path '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/ribbed_bracket.slvs'.

integrity.description: Agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the SolveSpace application interface with visible menus, toolbars, and property browser, not just a terminal window or text editor.
Across multiple screenshots, the model should evolve from a blank canvas to a 2D sketch, then a 3D extrusion, then the added rib, rather than appearing fully formed instantly.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply copy a pre-existing file
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands showing 'cp' or 'wget' of a file named 'ribbed_bracket.slvs' into the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/ribbed_bracket.slvs'. The final 3D model should have an exact volume of 57250 mm^3 (base: 52000 mm^3, rib: 5250 mm^3) with a tolerance of 2000 mm^3. The bounding box spans X: 0 to 60, Y: 0 to 80, and Z: -20 to 20 (due to symmetric extrusion of the 40mm width).
